allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking strengthens reframing-perspective by redirecting attention from surface faults to the interrelationships creating the problem.
allies.source_quote: Systems Thinking suggests reframing a broken element from a static fault to a damaged system.

allies.rationale_text: Trade-offs help reframing-perspective expose what different lenses make newly visible about success criteria, downside, and reversibility.
allies.source_quote: Best when the recommendation should actually change what counts as success, what variables matter, or what trade-offs become visible.

antagonists.rationale_text: Commitment-bias resists reframing-perspective by making teams cling to the old interpretation rather than adopt a better lens.
antagonists.source_quote: Humans have a strong pressure to remain consistent with previous commitments or interpretations.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Reframing-perspective conflicts with commitment-bias when commitment to a prior frame makes reframing feel like inconsistency rather than adaptation.
structured_tensions.source_quote: reframing-perspective conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when commitment to a prior frame makes reframing feel like inconsistency rather than adaptation.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Reframing-perspective conflicts with confirmation-bias when the new frame is selected to confirm a preferred conclusion rather than reveal genuinely alternative structure.
structured_tensions.source_quote: reframing-perspective conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when the new frame is selected to confirm a preferred conclusion rather than reveal genuinely alternative structure.

curation_notes.summary: Preserved the clearest system and trade-off allies plus one strong first-order antagonist.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader bias-neighbor sprawl beyond the clearest current-vocabulary seams.
open_questions: Bias-blind-spot is source-real nearby, but the first-order surface is cleanest with commitment-bias.
